content.problemStatement:
Ramu and his wife Lata are facing a cash crisis. They go to the nearby ATM to get some cash. There are **3** ATMs inside the same room. People are standing in queue outside, and go inside the room in groups of 3 to the ATMs, fetch their money and come out. Lata has an irrational fear in getting money from ATM that her ATM pin will somehow be stolen and all her money will be lost. So, she will always like to go into the room with Ramu. Ramu is standing at position **K** in line, immediately followed by Lata (i.e. at position **K + 1**).

Can you tell whether Ramu and Lata both will be able to get money in such a way that Lata does not feel insecure?


Input Format
The first line contains an integer **T** denoting the number of test cases. **T** test cases follow.


The only line of each test case contains two space separated integers **N** and **K**, where **N** denotes number of people in the queue, and **K** denotes the position of Ramu.



Output Format
For each test case, output "yes" or "no" correspondingly denoting whether they both will be able to get the money without Lata getting scared.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T denoting the number of test cases. Each test case consists of a single line containing two space-separated integers N and K, where N denotes the number of people in the queue, and K denotes the position of Ramu.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output 'yes' if Ramu and Lata can get money without Lata getting scared, and 'no' otherwise.

sampleTestCases.explanation: In the first test case, Ramu is at position 1 and Lata is at position 2. They will go into the room together in the very first batch of three. In the second test case, Ramu is at position 3 and Lata is at position 4. The first two people and Ramu will go into the room first. Then Lata and the remaining two people after her will go into the room, but Lata will be scared as Ramu is not there with her in the room.

solution.approach: To determine if Ramu and Lata can get money without Lata getting scared, we need to check if they will be in the same group of three people when they enter the room. We can do this by checking if the position of Ramu (K) is such that Lata (at position K + 1) will be in the same group. Since groups are formed every three people, we can use the modulo operator to find the remainder of (K - 1) divided by 3. If the remainder is 0 or 1, Ramu and Lata will be in the same group, and the answer is 'yes'. Otherwise, the answer is 'no'.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) — we are simply looping over T test cases and performing a constant amount of work for each case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — we are using a constant amount of space to store the input and output values.
